extraction.one_line_summary: POGAČAR attacked early on the Luz Ardiden climb, won the stage, and extended his GC lead to 5:45 while also claiming the polka dot jersey.
tactical_patterns: POGAČAR attacked unusually early on Luz Ardiden finale rather than waiting for steeper gradients, nullifying late-race counter tactics.
ALAPHILIPPE and PÉRICHON expended major watts bridging to early break of BENNETT/JUUL-JENSEN/MOHORIČ, signaling aggressive French intent post-Bastille Day.
INEOS riding tempo while POGAČAR was already clear suggests they prioritized limiting losses over contesting the stage win.
POGAČAR gained time bonuses plus stage gap, using mountaintop finish to extend lead beyond just climbing superiority.

rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: Won stage, extended GC lead to 5:45, took polka dot jersey—dominating all classifications with attacking style at age 22.
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: negative: Lost 6 additional seconds to POGAČAR despite being in second overall, unable to respond to early attack.
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: negative: Dropped to third overall, losing ground on both POGAČAR and VINGEGAARD on final Pyrenean mountaintop finish.
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: neutral: Made costly early effort to bridge to break but faded, showing willingness to gamble energy for stage hunt.
rider_signals.BENNETT Sean: positive: First Tour participation, initiated successful early break that held off peloton initially, strong debut performance.
rider_signals.MOHORIČ Matej: positive: Second stage win of this Tour confirmed, launching successful early move before mountaintop finish.
rider_signals.POELS Wout: negative: Lost polka dot jersey on final mountain stage after holding it three days, finished 21 points down to POGAČAR.
